title: AvatarUpload
description: An all-in-one avatar manager: upload a photo, crop it to a circle, and optionally generate one with AI. The AI provider is always external — you supply the function; mood-ui only handles the UI and state.

basic.title: Basic upload
basic.desc: Drop or click to pick a file. The built-in circular cropper opens automatically before committing the image.

noCrop.title: Without crop step
noCrop.desc: Set :crop="false" to skip the cropper and use the resized image directly.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes control the preview circle and icon scale.

saveMode.title: Save mode — manual
saveMode.desc: With saveMode="manual" a Save button appears once an image is ready. The onSave callback receives an AvatarSavePayload so you can upload to your backend and return the final URL.

withAi.title: AI generation (mock)
withAi.desc: Provide a :provider function that calls your own AI API and returns a URL. mood-ui never imports AI SDKs directly.

lightbox.title: Lightbox preview
lightbox.desc: Add :lightbox="true" to show an expand button on the avatar. Clicking it opens the image full-size in a modal — useful in profile cards, read-only views, and dashboards.

profileCard.title: Profile card
profileCard.desc: Horizontal layout with the avatar on the left and user info on the right — a common pattern in dashboards and profile pages.

settingsRow.title: Settings row
settingsRow.desc: Compact horizontal row used in account settings: avatar alongside a short description and the upload controls below.

inForm.title: Inside a form
inForm.desc: Avatar centered at the top of a profile form, with name and bio fields below — a typical onboarding pattern.

readonly.title: Read-only
readonly.desc: Displays the avatar without any upload or action controls.

props.lightbox: Show an expand button on hover. Clicking it opens the image full-size in a modal.
props.promptSuggestions: Quick-pick prompt chips shown above the text input. Selecting a chip sets the prompt; selecting it again clears it.
props.ai: Enable AI generation UI. Requires a provider function.
props.provider: Function called by mood-ui to generate an avatar. Receives \{ prompt, image, variant \} and an AbortSignal. Returns a Promise(string) (URL).
props.variant: Initial selected style variant for AI generation.
props.name: User name used as alt text and avatar initials fallback.
props.size: Size of the preview circle and icons.
props.crop: Open the circular crop modal after file selection. Default: true.
props.maxSize: Maximum file size in bytes. Default: 5 MB.
props.accept: Allowed MIME types. Default: PNG, JPEG, WebP.
props.regenerate: Show regenerate button after first AI generation.
props.promptPlaceholder: Custom placeholder for the prompt input.
props.saveMode: 'auto' calls onSave immediately. 'manual' shows a Save button. 'none' emits update:modelValue with the blob URL directly.
props.onSave: Async function that receives AvatarSavePayload and returns the final URL after uploading to your backend.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted with the final URL whenever the avatar changes.
emits.generated: Emitted with the URL returned by the provider after successful generation.
emits.uploaded: Emitted with the original File object after upload validation.
emits.error: Emitted with the Error on any failure (validation, generation, save).
emits.loading: Emitted with the loading boolean state.
emits.change: Emitted with the current URL or null whenever the preview changes.
emits.remove: Emitted when the avatar is removed.
emits.save: Emitted with AvatarSavePayload just before the onSave call.
emits.saved: Emitted with the final URL after onSave resolves.
emits.saveError: Emitted with the Error if onSave rejects.

integration.title: AI provider integration
integration.desc: mood-ui never ships an AI SDK — you bring your own. The component calls the provider function you supply; that function can call any API you want. The cleanest approach is a backend endpoint that holds your API keys.
integration.helperDesc: The included helper turns a URL into a ready-to-use provider. By default it POSTs \{ prompt, variant \} and auto-detects common response shapes (OpenAI, Replicate, or plain \{ url \}).
integration.backendNodeDesc: Create a POST endpoint that calls your AI service and returns \{ url \}. mood-ui fetches that URL and converts it to a local blob.
integration.uploadTitle: Saving the processed image to your backend
integration.uploadDesc: When onSave is provided, mood-ui calls it with an AvatarSavePayload containing a 512×512 WebP Blob. Upload it to S3, Cloudinary, Supabase Storage, etc. and return the final CDN URL.
integration.directTitle: Direct AI API calls (development / testing only)
integration.directDesc: You can call an AI API directly from the browser during development. Never do this in production — API keys in the frontend are public. Use a .env variable and exclude it from production builds.

types.title: Types
types.desc: TypeScript interfaces for AvatarUpload props, the provider function, and the save payload.

a11y.kbActivate: Activate the upload control or confirm action in a dialog
a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEsc: Close the crop modal or AI panel, restore focus to the trigger
a11y.ariaRole: Upload zone is exposed as a button so screen readers announce it as interactive
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the upload control when the user name is not set
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.alt: The user name prop is used as alt text on the avatar image for screen readers
a11y.ariaLive: Loading and error states are announced to screen readers via a polite live region
a11y.focusUpload: The upload zone is focusable and participates in the tab order as a native button
a11y.focusCrop: The crop modal traps focus while open; Tab cycles within the dialog
a11y.focusTrap: Focus returns to the upload trigger when the crop modal or AI panel is closed
a11y.focusDisabled: Excluded from the tab order when the disabled prop is true
